body_txt: An old franchise comes back after many years , but, of course, now the actors are noticeably older. The creators decide not to recast the roles or make the actors look younger with Digital De-Aging . Instead, they do a Real-Time Timeskip to acknowledge that years have passed and that the characters have aged — just like their actors. The writers may lampshade this by having the hero make a comment like "I'm getting too old for this," or may even have the entire plot of the story revolve around the characters Feeling Their Age . In certain cases, it can be Lampshaded , Hand Waved , and/or Invoked when the character is an ageless being/spirit/alien/robot/hologram/etc. that shouldn't have aged but provides some excuse for doing so anyway, often so as to fit in with the mere mortals they associate with . The trope can also be Averted if the character is only brought back for an animated series or other similar reprise that conspicuously avoids having to deal with the actor's aging, or else through Digital De-Aging (probably only practical for a brief appearance). Compare with Role Reprise . See also The Character Died with Him .
